payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\docs\mcp-integration.md
+# MCP and External Data Integration
+
+This document describes how Designflow should expose company context, business data,
+and images to other applications and AI coding/chat sessions.
+
+## Recommendation
+
+Build this as a hybrid:
+
+1. Keep Designflow's normal REST API as the source of truth for applications.
+2. Add a thin MCP server for AI clients.
+3. Have the MCP server call backend services or API-facing helpers instead of
+   duplicating business logic.
+
+This gives regular applications predictable HTTP endpoints while giving AI tools a
+standard way to ask questions like:
+
+- "How does RFQ pricing work here?"
+- "Find the current item details and attachments for price code X."
+- "What fields does the RFQ grid expose to vendors?"
+- "Fetch images for this item so I can reason about packaging/artwork."
+- "Explain the Designflow auth and deployment flow before changing code."
+
+## Why Not Only MCP?
+
+MCP is best for AI context and tool use. It is not a replacement for a public or
+partner API:
+
+- Other applications usually want stable REST endpoints, pagination, filtering,
+  webhooks, and service credentials.
+- AI sessions want discoverable tools, resources, prompts, and concise context.
+- Images and large binary assets should still be delivered through signed URLs,
+  not inline MCP payloads.
+
+The MCP server should expose carefully scoped views of Designflow rather than the
+entire database.
+
+## Recommended Shape
+
+```text
+External apps
+  -> Designflow REST API
+  -> JWT or service token auth
+  -> JSON data + signed image URLs
+
+AI clients / coding agents
+  -> Designflow MCP server
+  -> tools/resources/prompts
+  -> backend services + docs + signed image URLs
+
+Designflow MCP server
+  -> designflow-backend services/controllers
+  -> Postgres / DigitalOcean Spaces / app docs
+```
+
+## Where It Should Live
+
+Start in `designflow-backend`, because it already has:
+
+- JWT verification in `helpers/auth.js`
+- request context in `helpers/context.middleware.js`
+- Sequelize models and domain services
+- DigitalOcean Spaces helpers for images
+- docs and AGENTS guidance that AI sessions already need
+
+The first implementation can be a separate process under this repo, for example:
+
+```text
+mcp/
+  server.js
+  tools/
+  resources/
+  prompts/
+```
+
+Run it as its own Cloud Run service when remote AI clients need access. For local
+developer use, also support stdio transport so desktop AI tools can launch it.
+
+## Transport
+
+Use Streamable HTTP for a remote service. Use stdio for local/private developer
+sessions.
+
+The official MCP TypeScript SDK supports servers with tools/resources/prompts and
+Streamable HTTP or stdio transports. Keep the MCP runtime thin: transport handling
+belongs there; business logic stays in existing backend modules.
+
+## Authentication
+
+Do not make this an unauthenticated knowledge hose.
+
+Use one of these auth modes:
+
+- User-scoped: pass the user's existing Designflow JWT and apply the same role
+  limits as backend routes.
+- Service-scoped: use a dedicated service identity for trusted internal tools.
+  This should be read-only at first and should log every call.
+
+Initial MCP tools should be read-only. Write tools can come later, but only after
+we add explicit auditing, role checks, and narrow schemas.
+
+## First MCP Resources
+
+Resources are good for durable context that AI sessions read before coding.
+
+Suggested resources:
+
+| Resource | Purpose | Source |
+|---|---|---|
+| `designflow://docs/backend-agents` | Backend developer guide | `AGENTS.md` |
+| `designflow://docs/backend-architecture` | Route/module architecture | `docs/architecture.md` |
+| `designflow://docs/frontend-architecture` | Frontend structure | sibling frontend docs |
+| `designflow://schemas/rfq-item` | RFQ item fields and meanings | Sequelize model + curated notes |
+| `designflow://schemas/item-attachment` | Attachment/image field meanings | Sequelize model + DigitalOcean helper |
+| `designflow://company/process/rfq` | Company RFQ process summary | curated internal doc |
+| `designflow://company/process/sample-tracking` | Sample tracking process summary | curated internal doc |
+
+Avoid dumping raw model files into MCP as the main experience. Curated resources
+are better for AI because they preserve company language, edge cases, and "why"
+behind the code.
+
+## First MCP Tools
+
+Tools are good for current data lookups.
+
+Start with read-only tools:
+
+| Tool | Input | Output |
+|---|---|---|
+| `search_items` | text query, status filters, limit | item ids, price codes, descriptions, customer, image count |
+| `get_item` | item id or price code | item header/detail, vendors, attachments, signed image URLs |
+| `get_item_images` | item id or attachment ids | signed URLs and metadata |
+| `search_rfq_items` | query, merch group, customer, status, limit | RFQ rows summarized for AI |
+| `get_rfq_item` | RFQ item id or price code | RFQ item, vendor quotes, status, history summary |
+| `get_grid_layout` | grid name and role | columns visible/editable by that role |
+| `search_factories` | text query, country/group filters | factory ids, names, country, active state |
+| `get_company_context` | topic enum | curated explanation of workflows and code ownership |
+
+Each tool should return compact JSON. For huge grids, return summaries and include
+pagination cursors.
+
+## First MCP Prompts
+
+Prompts help AI sessions ask good internal questions consistently.
+
+Suggested prompts:
+
+- `prepare_designflow_code_change`: loads backend/frontend architecture, route
+  ownership, auth rules, and the relevant process note.
+- `investigate_rfq_behavior`: asks for price code or RFQ item id, then gathers
+  item, vendor, quote, grid-layout, and audit context.
+- `inspect_item_images`: gathers item attachments and signed URLs, then asks the
+  AI to reason from both data and images.
+
+## REST API Additions
+
+Before or alongside MCP, add a small external read API:
+
+```text
+GET /api/external/items/search?q=&limit=&cursor=
+GET /api/external/items/:id
+GET /api/external/items/:id/images
+GET /api/external/rfq-items/search?q=&status=&limit=&cursor=
+GET /api/external/rfq-items/:id
+GET /api/external/grid-layouts/:name?role=
+```
+
+These endpoints should use stable DTOs instead of returning raw Sequelize rows.
+That protects external callers from internal column churn and lets MCP reuse the
+same response shape.
+
+## Image Handling
+
+Return image metadata plus signed URLs:
+
+```json
+{
+  "id": "attachment-uuid",
+  "kind": "product-photo",
+  "fileName": "abc.png",
+  "contentType": "image/png",
+  "url": "https://...",
+  "expiresAt": "2026-06-08T20:00:00.000Z"
+}
+```
+
+Do not stream large image bytes through MCP unless a client specifically requires
+that format. Signed URLs are easier to cache, inspect, revoke, and audit.
+
+## Security Defaults
+
+- Read-only first.
+- Return least-privilege data based on role.
+- Redact vendor/customer-sensitive fields unless explicitly needed.
+- Log tool name, caller identity, params summary, result count, and duration.
+- Add rate limits for search tools.
+- Avoid broad SQL-like filters from clients; use typed filters.
+- Never expose raw database credentials or env vars through resources.
+
+## Implementation Phases
+
+### Phase 1: Internal AI Context
+
+- Add MCP resources for backend/frontend docs.
+- Add `get_company_context`.
+- Add `get_grid_layout`.
+- Keep everything read-only.
+
+### Phase 2: Data and Images
+
+- Add stable external DTO service methods.
+- Add `search_items`, `get_item`, `get_item_images`.
+- Return signed URLs for image access.
+
+### Phase 3: RFQ and Workflow Intelligence
+
+- Add `search_rfq_items` and `get_rfq_item`.
+- Include quote status, vendor visibility rules, and audit summaries.
+- Add prompts for RFQ investigations.
+
+### Phase 4: Partner/API Hardening
+
+- Add `/api/external/*` routes.
+- Add service-token auth and rate limiting.
+- Publish an OpenAPI spec.
+- Add webhook events only after consumers exist.
+
+## Testing
+
+Add tests at three layers:
+
+- unit tests for DTO builders and auth filtering
+- MCP tool tests with mocked service calls
+- integration tests for `/api/external/*` routes once added
+
+For image tools, assert that responses contain signed URLs and metadata, not raw
+binary payloads.
+
+## Open Decisions
+
+- Which external applications need access first?
+- Should MCP callers act as real users or as a single internal service account?
+- Which data is allowed to leave Designflow for AI clients?
+- How long should signed image URLs live?
+- Do we need customer/vendor-specific data walls for AI sessions?
+
*** End Patch

# MCP and External Data Integration

This document describes how Designflow should expose company context, business data,
and images to other applications and AI coding/chat sessions.

## Recommendation

Build this as a hybrid:

1. Keep Designflow's normal REST API as the source of truth for applications.
2. Add a thin MCP server for AI clients.
3. Have the MCP server call backend services or API-facing helpers instead of
   duplicating business logic.

This gives regular applications predictable HTTP endpoints while giving AI tools a
standard way to ask questions like:

- "How does RFQ pricing work here?"
- "Find the current item details and attachments for price code X."
- "What fields does the RFQ grid expose to vendors?"
- "Fetch images for this item so I can reason about packaging/artwork."
- "Explain the Designflow auth and deployment flow before changing code."

## Why Not Only MCP?

MCP is best for AI context and tool use. It is not a replacement for a public or
partner API:

- Other applications usually want stable REST endpoints, pagination, filtering,
  webhooks, and service credentials.
- AI sessions want discoverable tools, resources, prompts, and concise context.
- Images and large binary assets should still be delivered through signed URLs,
  not inline MCP payloads.

The MCP server should expose carefully scoped views of Designflow rather than the
entire database.

## Recommended Shape

```text
External apps
  -> Designflow REST API
  -> JWT or service token auth
  -> JSON data + signed image URLs

AI clients / coding agents
  -> Designflow MCP server
  -> tools/resources/prompts
  -> backend services + docs + signed image URLs

Designflow MCP server
  -> designflow-backend services/controllers
  -> Postgres / DigitalOcean Spaces / app docs
```

## Where It Should Live

Start in `designflow-backend`, because it already has:

- JWT verification in `helpers/auth.js`
- request context in `helpers/context.middleware.js`
- Sequelize models and domain services
- DigitalOcean Spaces helpers for images
- docs and AGENTS guidance that AI sessions already need

The first implementation can be a separate process under this repo, for example:

```text
mcp/
  server.js
  tools/
  resources/
  prompts/
```

Run it as its own Cloud Run service when remote AI clients need access. For local
developer use, also support stdio transport so desktop AI tools can launch it.

## Transport

Use Streamable HTTP for a remote service. Use stdio for local/private developer
sessions.

The official MCP TypeScript SDK supports servers with tools/resources/prompts and
Streamable HTTP or stdio transports. Keep the MCP runtime thin: transport handling
belongs there; business logic stays in existing backend modules.

## Authentication

Do not make this an unauthenticated knowledge hose.

Use one of these auth modes:

- User-scoped: pass the user's existing Designflow JWT and apply the same role
  limits as backend routes.
- Service-scoped: use a dedicated service identity for trusted internal tools.
  This should be read-only at first and should log every call.

Initial MCP tools should be read-only. Write tools can come later, but only after
we add explicit auditing, role checks, and narrow schemas.

## First MCP Resources

Resources are good for durable context that AI sessions read before coding.

Suggested resources:

| Resource | Purpose | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `designflow://docs/backend-agents` | Backend developer guide | `AGENTS.md` |
| `designflow://docs/backend-architecture` | Route/module architecture | `docs/architecture.md` |
| `designflow://docs/frontend-architecture` | Frontend structure | sibling frontend docs |
| `designflow://schemas/rfq-item` | RFQ item fields and meanings | Sequelize model + curated notes |
| `designflow://schemas/item-attachment` | Attachment/image field meanings | Sequelize model + DigitalOcean helper |
| `designflow://company/process/rfq` | Company RFQ process summary | curated internal doc |
| `designflow://company/process/sample-tracking` | Sample tracking process summary | curated internal doc |

Avoid dumping raw model files into MCP as the main experience. Curated resources
are better for AI because they preserve company language, edge cases, and "why"
behind the code.

## First MCP Tools

Tools are good for current data lookups.

Start with read-only tools:

| Tool | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|
| `search_items` | text query, status filters, limit | item ids, price codes, descriptions, customer, image count |
| `get_item` | item id or price code | item header/detail, vendors, attachments, signed image URLs |
| `get_item_images` | item id or attachment ids | signed URLs and metadata |
| `search_rfq_items` | query, merch group, customer, status, limit | RFQ rows summarized for AI |
| `get_rfq_item` | RFQ item id or price code | RFQ item, vendor quotes, status, history summary |
| `get_grid_layout` | grid name and role | columns visible/editable by that role |
| `search_factories` | text query, country/group filters | factory ids, names, country, active state |
| `get_company_context` | topic enum | curated explanation of workflows and code ownership |

Each tool should return compact JSON. For huge grids, return summaries and include
pagination cursors.

## First MCP Prompts

Prompts help AI sessions ask good internal questions consistently.

Suggested prompts:

- `prepare_designflow_code_change`: loads backend/frontend architecture, route
  ownership, auth rules, and the relevant process note.
- `investigate_rfq_behavior`: asks for price code or RFQ item id, then gathers
  item, vendor, quote, grid-layout, and audit context.
- `inspect_item_images`: gathers item attachments and signed URLs, then asks the
  AI to reason from both data and images.

## REST API Additions

Before or alongside MCP, add a small external read API:

```text
GET /api/external/items/search?q=&limit=&cursor=
GET /api/external/items/:id
GET /api/external/items/:id/images
GET /api/external/rfq-items/search?q=&status=&limit=&cursor=
GET /api/external/rfq-items/:id
GET /api/external/grid-layouts/:name?role=
```

These endpoints should use stable DTOs instead of returning raw Sequelize rows.
That protects external callers from internal column churn and lets MCP reuse the
same response shape.

## Image Handling

Return image metadata plus signed URLs:

```json
{
  "id": "attachment-uuid",
  "kind": "product-photo",
  "fileName": "abc.png",
  "contentType": "image/png",
  "url": "https://...",
  "expiresAt": "2026-06-08T20:00:00.000Z"
}
```

Do not stream large image bytes through MCP unless a client specifically requires
that format. Signed URLs are easier to cache, inspect, revoke, and audit.

## Security Defaults

- Read-only first.
- Return least-privilege data based on role.
- Redact vendor/customer-sensitive fields unless explicitly needed.
- Log tool name, caller identity, params summary, result count, and duration.
- Add rate limits for search tools.
- Avoid broad SQL-like filters from clients; use typed filters.
- Never expose raw database credentials or env vars through resources.

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Internal AI Context

- Add MCP resources for backend/frontend docs.
- Add `get_company_context`.
- Add `get_grid_layout`.
- Keep everything read-only.

### Phase 2: Data and Images

- Add stable external DTO service methods.
- Add `search_items`, `get_item`, `get_item_images`.
- Return signed URLs for image access.

### Phase 3: RFQ and Workflow Intelligence

- Add `search_rfq_items` and `get_rfq_item`.
- Include quote status, vendor visibility rules, and audit summaries.
- Add prompts for RFQ investigations.

### Phase 4: Partner/API Hardening

- Add `/api/external/*` routes.
- Add service-token auth and rate limiting.
- Publish an OpenAPI spec.
- Add webhook events only after consumers exist.

## Testing

Add tests at three layers:

- unit tests for DTO builders and auth filtering
- MCP tool tests with mocked service calls
- integration tests for `/api/external/*` routes once added

For image tools, assert that responses contain signed URLs and metadata, not raw
binary payloads.

## Open Decisions

- Which external applications need access first?
- Should MCP callers act as real users or as a single internal service account?
- Which data is allowed to leave Designflow for AI clients?
- How long should signed image URLs live?
- Do we need customer/vendor-specific data walls for AI sessions?

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

Use the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

Output:
# Designflow BFF

Backend-for-Frontend service for Designflow. Provides a single authenticated proxy entry point for the Angular SPA and forwards requests to the correct backend service (`core`, `item_master`, `tracking`, `data_sync`).

---

## Stack

- Node.js + Express
- `http-proxy` for request forwarding
- `google-auth-library` for Cloud Run OIDC token generation

---

## How it works

```
Browser → BFF (api.*.designflow.app)
  └── BFF attaches Google OIDC token → backend Cloud Run service
        └── Backend verifies token, serves request
```

The BFF runs on Cloud Run as a service account (`designflow-bff-invoker@...` for sandbox environments, `deployer@...` for production). Both have `roles/run.invoker` at the GCP project level, so they can call any backend service.

For every proxied request, `src/backendProxy.js`:
1. Fetches an OIDC identity token from the GCP metadata server for the target backend service URL
2. Sets `Authorization: Bearer <oidc-token>` (for Cloud Run authentication)
3. Moves the original user JWT to `X-User-Authorization` (the backend remaps this back to `Authorization` in its first middleware)

---

## Critical: `*_BACKEND_URL` must be the `.run.app` service URL

**Do not set any `*_BACKEND_URL` to a custom domain (e.g. `https://api.core.*.designflow.app`).**

Cloud Run verifies an incoming OIDC token by checking its `aud` claim against the service's URL. In practice, this means the `*.run.app` URL — **not** a custom domain mapped to the service. Even though Cloud Run domain mappings are configured and the custom domain resolves correctly, using a custom domain as the OIDC token audience results in:

```
The request was not authorized to invoke this service. The access token could not be verified.
```

This caused the 2026-05-26 SSO outage (see `AGENTS.md` §Incidents). All three environments were updated to use `.run.app` URLs.

---

## Project layout

```
src/server.js           App bootstrap, CORS, env validation
src/serviceMap.js       Maps service keys → backend URLs from env
src/backendProxy.js     OIDC token attachment; header swap logic
routes/api.js           Generic route /api/:service/*
routes/health.js        GET /health
routes/index.js         Mounts api and health routers
config/env.js           Loads .env.${NODE_ENV}
env/                    Per-environment .env files
  .env.sandbox-albert   Albert's sandbox
  .env.sandbox          Shared sandbox
  .env.production       Production
```

---

## Cloud Run services and URLs

| Environment | BFF service | BFF domain | Core backend service | Core backend URL (for OIDC) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Albert's sandbox | `popcre-albert-bff-sandbox` | `api.alsand.designflow.app` | `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` | `https://popcre-albert-backend-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app` |
| Shared sandbox | `popcre-bff-sandbox` | `api.sandbox.designflow.app` | `popcre-core-sandbox` | `https://popcre-core-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app` |
| Production | `popcre-bff-prod` | `api.designflow.app` | `popcre-core-prod` | `https://popcre-core-prod-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app` |

GCP project: `lithe-breaker-323913`, region: `us-central1`.

If a backend service is redeployed and gets a new `.run.app` URL, update the Cloud Run env var via:
```bash
gcloud run services update <bff-service> \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 \
  --update-env-vars CORE_BACKEND_URL=https://<new-url>.run.app
```
And update the corresponding `env/.env.*` file.

---

## Local development

```bash
npm install
cp env/.env.example env/.env.local   # fill in real values; use localhost backend URLs
NODE_ENV=local node src/server.js
```

Local `.env` files with `http://localhost:*` backend URLs automatically skip OIDC token generation (`isLocalHttpBackend` check in `src/backendProxy.js`).

---

## Run (Cloud environments)

```bash
npm run start:sandbox-albert   # Albert's sandbox
npm run start:sandbox          # Shared sandbox
npm run start:production       # Production
```

Each script runs `npm test` first, then starts `nodemon`. Tests must pass or the process exits.

---

## Tests

```bash
npm test                          # Jest unit tests (tests/**/*.test.js)
npx jest --watch                  # Watch mode
npx jest tests/unit/sanity.test.js  # Single file
```

---

## API contract

- `GET /health` → `{ status: 'ok' }`
- `ALL /api/:service/*` → proxied to the backend identified by `:service`

Valid service keys: `core`, `item_master`, `tracking`, `data_sync`. Unknown key → 404.

Route example: `POST /api/core/auth/azure` → proxied to `<CORE_BACKEND_URL>/auth/azure`.
{
  "name": "designflow-bff",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Backend for Frontend for DesignFlow",
  "main": "src/server.js",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "npm test && cross-env NODE_ENV=localhost nodemon src/server.js",
    "start:sandbox": "npm test && cross-env NODE_ENV=sandbox nodemon src/server.js",
    "start:sandbox-albert": "npm test && cross-env NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert nodemon src/server.js",
    "start:production": "npm test && node src/server.js",
    "test:proxy": "node test/proxyServer.test.js",
    "test:token": "node test/tokenGeneration.test.js",
    "test:smoke": "npm run test:token && npm run test:proxy",
    "test": "jest",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui",
    "test:e2e:report": "playwright show-report"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "popcre",
    "bff",
    "backend for frontend"
  ],
  "author": "Albert Hazon",
  "license": "ISC",
  "dependencies": {
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "dotenv": "^17.2.3",
    "express": "^4.19.0",
    "google-auth-library": "^9.0.0",
    "http-proxy": "^1.18.1",
    "jest": "^29.7.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@playwright/test": "^1.59.1",
    "cross-env": "^7.0.3",
    "nodemon": "^3.1.9"
  }
}
const express = require("express");
const cors = require("cors");
const { env, envFile } = require("../config/env.js");
const { SERVICE_MAP } = require("./serviceMap.js");
const routes = require("../routes/index.js");

const app = express();

const corsOrigins = process.env.CORS_ORIGINS
  ? process.env.CORS_ORIGINS.split(/[|,]/).map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)
  : true;

app.use(
  cors({
    origin: corsOrigins,
    credentials: true,
    allowedHeaders: [
      "Authorization",
      "Content-Type",
      "X-API-Key",
      "X-Enable-Loader",
      "Accept",
    ],
  })
);

const port = process.env.PORT || 5004;

// Fail fast if critical env vars are missing
Object.entries(SERVICE_MAP).forEach(([service, url]) => {
  if (!url) {
    console.warn(`[WARN] Backend URL not set for service: ${service}`);
  }
});

app.use(routes);

function startServer() {
  app.listen(port, "0.0.0.0", function () {
    console.log(`🚀 Server running on port ${port} in the ${env} environment`);
    console.log(`🧠 API Address: ${process.env.API_ADDRESS}`);
    console.log(`⚙️  Env File: ${envFile}`);
  });
}

startServer();
const express = require("express");
const apiRoutes = require("./api");
const healthRoutes = require("./health");

const router = express.Router();

// Health check (no /api prefix)
router.use("/", healthRoutes);

// API proxy routes
router.use("/api", apiRoutes);

module.exports = router;

Output:
# Designflow Frontend

Angular 21 SPA for the Designflow PLM platform. Covers the full product lifecycle: RFQ (vendor quoting and margin calculation), Item Library, Standardized Products, Art Piece management, and Production/Licensing Tracking.

**Before working on this codebase, read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md).**

---

## Branch workflow

Work only on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding code, sync GitHub `develop` into GitHub `sandbox-albert`, pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally, then make changes. Push commits to `sandbox-albert` and keep the PR from `sandbox-albert` to `develop` updated or create it if it is missing. Never merge to `main`.

Add or update unit tests for new behavior before committing.

---

## Quick start

```bash
# Install (uses yarn lockfile — do not use npm install)
yarn install

# Serve locally (dev environment, port 4200)
npm run start

# Run tests
npm run test

# Lint
npm run lint
```

## Builds

| Command | Environment | API target |
|---|---|---|
| `npm run build:production` | production | `api.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:develop` | staging | `api.sandbox.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:sandbox-albert` | sandbox (Albert) | `api.alsand.designflow.app` |

## Key directories

| Path | Contents |
|---|---|
| `src/app/pages/` | Feature modules (RFQ, item library, tracking, admin) |
| `src/app/helpers/` | API service, models, interceptors, AG Grid column configs |
| `src/environments/` | Per-environment API base URLs |
| `docs/` | Architecture, development, configuration, deployment guides |

## Documentation

Start with [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md). The focused guides live in [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md), [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md), [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md), and [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md).

## Stack

- **Angular 21.1.3** with Angular Material and CDK
- **AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1** — primary data grid for all feature modules
- **RxJS 7.8.1** — all async operations use `firstValueFrom()` (not `.toPromise()`)
- **PrimeNG 19.1.4** — supplementary UI components
- **Tailwind CSS 2.0.4**
- **@auth0/angular-jwt** — JWT token handling
- **@azure/msal-browser 5.x** — Microsoft SSO (Azure AD popup login)
- **nginx-brotli** — production serving with brotli compression
- **Google Cloud Run** — deployment target

payload.message:
Yes. Designflow already has an API system.

The shape is:

- **Frontend** calls one gateway: `https://api.designflow.app/api/...`
  See [environment.prod.ts](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend/src/environments/environment.prod.ts:3).

- **BFF/API gateway** receives those calls at `/api/:service/*` and proxies to backend services.
  Valid service keys are `core`, `item_master`, `tracking`, and `data_sync`.
  See [designflow-bff README](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-bff/README.md:88) and [routes/index.js](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-bff/routes/index.js:11).

- **Core backend API** exists and is large: RFQ, vendors, customers, grid layouts, comments, admin settings, auth, files/images, OpenRouter/CROSS, notifications.
  See [backend routes](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-backend/routes/index.js:5).

- **Item Master API** exists: item library, item detail, art pieces.
  See [item-master routes](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-item-master/routes/index.js:4).

- **Tracking API** exists: production tracking, licensing, lead-time, sample tracking, sample photos.
  See [tracking routes](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-tracking/routes/index.js:4).

- **Data Sync API** exists: system-to-system Coldlion/designflow sync, protected by `X-API-Key`.
  See [data-sync README](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-data-syncing/README.md:1) and [index.js](D:/repos/dflow/designflow-data-syncing/index.js:24).

So the answer is: **yes, you already have APIs, plural.** What you do *not* appear to have yet is a clean, documented, external-facing API layer with stable DTOs/OpenAPI docs for other apps, or an MCP server for AI sessions. Right now it’s mostly an internal app API behind the BFF, with lots of routes shaped around the Angular frontend’s needs.

I couldn’t inspect `popcre/infrastructure` locally because it is not checked out under `D:\repos\dflow`; the GitHub link also wasn’t publicly readable from here.

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\repos\dflow\API_INVENTORY.md
+# Designflow API Inventory
+
+This workspace contains a multi-service API system. The Angular frontend does not
+call the backend services directly in deployed environments. It calls the BFF, and
+the BFF proxies each request to the correct backend service.
+
+## Production Entry Point
+
+| Caller | Base URL |
+|---|---|
+| Browser / frontend | `https://api.designflow.app` |
+| Local frontend | `http://localhost:5004` |
+
+The BFF exposes:
+
+```text
+/api/core/*        -> designflow-backend
+/api/item_master/* -> designflow-item-master
+/api/tracking/*    -> designflow-tracking
+/api/data_sync/*   -> designflow-data-syncing
+/health            -> BFF health
+```
+
+## Services
+
+| Repo | API role | Auth model | Local default port |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `designflow-bff` | Public API gateway for the SPA | User JWT from browser plus Cloud Run OIDC to services | `5004` |
+| `designflow-backend` | Core PLM/RFQ/admin/auth/file API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5000` |
+| `designflow-item-master` | Item library, item detail, art piece API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5003` |
+| `designflow-tracking` | Production, licensing, lead-time, sample tracking API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5002` |
+| `designflow-data-syncing` | Coldlion/system sync API | `X-API-Key` | `5001` |
+| `designflow-frontend` | API consumer, not an API provider | Sends JWT to the BFF | `4200` |
+| `infrastructure` | Deployment/IaC repo | Not checked out locally | n/a |
+
+## Important Boundary
+
+Designflow already has APIs, but they are mostly internal application APIs shaped
+around the Angular frontend. They are not yet a polished external/partner API:
+
+- no single OpenAPI spec
+- inconsistent route naming across legacy endpoints
+- many request/response bodies are raw domain rows or grid-specific payloads
+- BFF routes are stable as a gateway, but backend route contracts are not all
+  documented as public contracts
+
+For external applications, add a stable `/api/external/*` surface with DTOs.
+For AI sessions, add an MCP server that calls the existing APIs/services through
+read-only, role-aware tools.
+
+## Service API Docs
+
+| Repo | API doc |
+|---|---|
+| `designflow-bff` | `designflow-bff/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-backend` | `designflow-backend/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-item-master` | `designflow-item-master/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-tracking` | `designflow-tracking/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-data-syncing` | `designflow-data-syncing/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-frontend` | `designflow-frontend/docs/api-consumption.md` |
+
*** Add File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-bff\docs\api-reference.md
+# Designflow BFF API Reference
+
+The BFF is the public HTTP entry point for the Angular SPA. It proxies requests
+to private backend Cloud Run services and handles service-to-service identity.
+
+## Base URLs
+
+| Environment | Base URL |
+|---|---|
+| Local | `http://localhost:5004` |
+| Albert sandbox | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app` |
+| Shared sandbox | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app` |
+| Production | `https://api.designflow.app` |
+
+## Auth
+
+Browser callers send the user JWT in `Authorization`.
+
+For proxied requests, `src/backendProxy.js`:
+
+1. fetches a Google OIDC token for the target Cloud Run service
+2. sets `Authorization` to the OIDC token
+3. moves the original user JWT to `X-User-Authorization`
+
+Backend services then copy `X-User-Authorization` back to `Authorization` before
+running their normal JWT role checks.
+
+## Routes
+
+| Method | Path | Description |
+|---|---|---|
+| `GET` | `/health` | BFF health check |
+| `ALL` | `/api/:service/*` | Proxy to the backend service identified by `:service` |
+
+## Service Keys
+
+| Service key | Environment variable | Target repo |
+|---|---|---|
+| `core` | `CORE_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-backend` |
+| `item_master` | `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-item-master` |
+| `tracking` | `TRACKING_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-tracking` |
+| `data_sync` | `DATA_SYNC_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-data-syncing` |
+
+## Examples
+
+```text
+POST /api/core/auth/azure
+GET  /api/core/api/customers
+POST /api/core/getAllItem
+GET  /api/item_master/lib/getAllitems
+POST /api/item_master/detail/getItemInfo
+GET  /api/tracking/sample/list
+GET  /api/data_sync/getItemHeaderFromCL
+```
+
+Unknown service keys return `404`. Requests with no backend path also return
+`404`.
+
+## Contract Status
+
+This is a gateway contract. The BFF path shape is stable, but request and response
+bodies are owned by the downstream services.
+
*** Add File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\api-consumption.md
+# Designflow Frontend API Consumption
+
+The frontend is not an API provider. It is an Angular SPA that consumes the
+Designflow API through the BFF.
+
+## Environment Base URLs
+
+| Environment file | Core | Item master | Tracking | Data sync |
+|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `environment.ts` | `http://localhost:5004/api/core` | `http://localhost:5004/api/item_master` | `http://localhost:5004/api/tracking` | `http://localhost:5004/api/data_sync` |
+| `environment.sandbox.ts` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
+| `environment.staging.ts` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
+| `environment.prod.ts` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
+
+## Main API Service Consumers
+
+| Frontend service | Backend base | Purpose |
+|---|---|---|
+| `main.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Core RFQ, vendors, grid layout, files, comments, notifications |
+| `std.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Standardized product endpoints |
+| `admin.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Admin reference data |
+| `ai-settings.service.ts` | `environment.url` | AI model settings, tariffs, factory country map |
+| `cross.service.ts` | `environment.url` | CBP CROSS search |
+| `customer.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Customer CRUD and customer logo upload |
+| `item_library.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /lib` | Item library |
+| `item_detail.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /detail` | Item detail |
+| `art_piece.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /artPiece` | Art pieces |
+| `tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking` | Production and sample tracking |
+| `licensing_tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking + /lic` | Licensing tracking |
+| `sync.service.ts` | `environment.data_sync` | Data sync calls |
+
+## Auth
+
+Frontend services attach the user JWT in `Authorization`. The BFF preserves that
+user token as `X-User-Authorization` while it sends its Cloud Run OIDC token to
+backend services.
+
*** End Patch

D:\\repos\\dflow\\designflow-bff\\docs\\.content:
# Designflow BFF API Reference

The BFF is the public HTTP entry point for the Angular SPA. It proxies requests
to private backend Cloud Run services and handles service-to-service identity.

## Base URLs

| Environment | Base URL |
|---|---|
| Local | `http://localhost:5004` |
| Albert sandbox | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app` |
| Shared sandbox | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app` |
| Production | `https://api.designflow.app` |

## Auth

Browser callers send the user JWT in `Authorization`.

For proxied requests, `src/backendProxy.js`:

1. fetches a Google OIDC token for the target Cloud Run service
2. sets `Authorization` to the OIDC token
3. moves the original user JWT to `X-User-Authorization`

Backend services then copy `X-User-Authorization` back to `Authorization` before
running their normal JWT role checks.

## Routes

| Method | Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/health` | BFF health check |
| `ALL` | `/api/:service/*` | Proxy to the backend service identified by `:service` |

## Service Keys

| Service key | Environment variable | Target repo |
|---|---|---|
| `core` | `CORE_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-backend` |
| `item_master` | `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-item-master` |
| `tracking` | `TRACKING_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-tracking` |
| `data_sync` | `DATA_SYNC_BACKEND_URL` | `designflow-data-syncing` |

## Examples

```text
POST /api/core/auth/azure
GET  /api/core/api/customers
POST /api/core/getAllItem
GET  /api/item_master/lib/getAllitems
POST /api/item_master/detail/getItemInfo
GET  /api/tracking/sample/list
GET  /api/data_sync/getItemHeaderFromCL
```

Unknown service keys return `404`. Requests with no backend path also return
`404`.

## Contract Status

This is a gateway contract. The BFF path shape is stable, but request and response
bodies are owned by the downstream services.

D:\\repos\\dflow\\designflow-frontend\\d.content:
# Designflow Frontend API Consumption

The frontend is not an API provider. It is an Angular SPA that consumes the
Designflow API through the BFF.

## Environment Base URLs

| Environment file | Core | Item master | Tracking | Data sync |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `environment.ts` | `http://localhost:5004/api/core` | `http://localhost:5004/api/item_master` | `http://localhost:5004/api/tracking` | `http://localhost:5004/api/data_sync` |
| `environment.sandbox.ts` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
| `environment.staging.ts` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
| `environment.prod.ts` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |

## Main API Service Consumers

| Frontend service | Backend base | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `main.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Core RFQ, vendors, grid layout, files, comments, notifications |
| `std.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Standardized product endpoints |
| `admin.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Admin reference data |
| `ai-settings.service.ts` | `environment.url` | AI model settings, tariffs, factory country map |
| `cross.service.ts` | `environment.url` | CBP CROSS search |
| `customer.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Customer CRUD and customer logo upload |
| `item_library.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /lib` | Item library |
| `item_detail.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /detail` | Item detail |
| `art_piece.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /artPiece` | Art pieces |
| `tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking` | Production and sample tracking |
| `licensing_tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking + /lic` | Licensing tracking |
| `sync.service.ts` | `environment.data_sync` | Data sync calls |

## Auth

Frontend services attach the user JWT in `Authorization`. The BFF preserves that
user token as `X-User-Authorization` while it sends its Cloud Run OIDC token to
backend services.

D:\\repos\\dflow\\API_INVENTORY.md.content:
# Designflow API Inventory

This workspace contains a multi-service API system. The Angular frontend does not
call the backend services directly in deployed environments. It calls the BFF, and
the BFF proxies each request to the correct backend service.

## Production Entry Point

| Caller | Base URL |
|---|---|
| Browser / frontend | `https://api.designflow.app` |
| Local frontend | `http://localhost:5004` |

The BFF exposes:

```text
/api/core/*        -> designflow-backend
/api/item_master/* -> designflow-item-master
/api/tracking/*    -> designflow-tracking
/api/data_sync/*   -> designflow-data-syncing
/health            -> BFF health
```

## Services

| Repo | API role | Auth model | Local default port |
|---|---|---|---|
| `designflow-bff` | Public API gateway for the SPA | User JWT from browser plus Cloud Run OIDC to services | `5004` |
| `designflow-backend` | Core PLM/RFQ/admin/auth/file API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5000` |
| `designflow-item-master` | Item library, item detail, art piece API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5003` |
| `designflow-tracking` | Production, licensing, lead-time, sample tracking API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5002` |
| `designflow-data-syncing` | Coldlion/system sync API | `X-API-Key` | `5001` |
| `designflow-frontend` | API consumer, not an API provider | Sends JWT to the BFF | `4200` |
| `infrastructure` | Deployment/IaC repo | Not checked out locally | n/a |

## Important Boundary

Designflow already has APIs, but they are mostly internal application APIs shaped
around the Angular frontend. They are not yet a polished external/partner API:

- no single OpenAPI spec
- inconsistent route naming across legacy endpoints
- many request/response bodies are raw domain rows or grid-specific payloads
- BFF routes are stable as a gateway, but backend route contracts are not all
  documented as public contracts

For external applications, add a stable `/api/external/*` surface with DTOs.
For AI sessions, add an MCP server that calls the existing APIs/services through
read-only, role-aware tools.

## Service API Docs

| Repo | API doc |
|---|---|
| `designflow-bff` | `designflow-bff/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-backend` | `designflow-backend/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-item-master` | `designflow-item-master/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-tracking` | `designflow-tracking/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-data-syncing` | `designflow-data-syncing/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-frontend` | `designflow-frontend/docs/api-consumption.md` |

+# Designflow Core Backend API Reference
+
+This service is the core Designflow PLM API. It handles RFQ workflows, vendors,
+customers, grid layouts, admin settings, auth, files/images, comments,
+notifications, tariff lookups, CBP CROSS lookup, and AI proxy calls.
+
+In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:
+
+```text
+https://api.designflow.app/api/core/<core-backend-path>
+```
+
+Direct local base:
+
+```text
+http://localhost:5000
+```
+
+## Auth
+
+Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
+`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
+`X-User-Authorization`, and the backend remaps it to `Authorization`.
+
+Common roles:
+
+```text
+admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
+```
+
+Routes without `authRole(...)` are either login/token routes, webhooks, or legacy
+utility routes. Treat unauthenticated legacy routes as internal unless explicitly
+approved for external use.
+
+## Route Mounts
+
+| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
+|---|---|---|
+| `/` | `lib.router.js` | Legacy RFQ/product/vendor/grid/file endpoints |
+| `/std` | `std.router.js` | Standardized product workflow |
+| `/api/grid` | `grid.router.js` | Grid access metadata |
+| `/api/customers` | `customer.router.js` | Customer CRUD |
+| `/api/item-size` | `itemSize.router.js` | Item size CRUD |
+| `/api/item_depth` | `itemDepth.router.js` | Item depth CRUD |
+| `/` | `digitalocean.router.js` | File/image storage helpers |
+| `/api/cross` | `cross.router.js` | CBP CROSS search proxy |
+| `/api/openrouter` | `openrouter.router.js` | AI provider/model proxy |
+| `/api/comment` | `comment.router.js` | Comments |
+| `/api/role` | `role.router.js` | Role and UI permission metadata |
+| `/api/admin` | `admin.router.js` | Admin reference data, tariffs, settings, impersonation |
+| `/api/autofill` | `autofill.router.js` | Item autofill data |
+| `/api/user` | `user.router.js` | User profile/photo |
+| `/auth` | `auth.router.js` | Azure SSO |
+| `/api/notifications` | `notification.router.js` | Notifications |
+| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |
+
+## Health and Auth
+
+| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |
+| `POST` | `/auth/azure` | none | Azure SSO callback/login |
+| `GET` | `/verifyToken` | user JWT | Verify current token |
+| `POST` | `/verifySingleUseToken` | none | Verify single-use vendor/signup token |
+| `POST` | `/signup` | none | Signup flow |
+| `POST` | `/forgotPassw` | none | Forgot password flow |
+| `POST` | `/changePassword` | none | Password change flow |
+

+
+## Reference and Utility Routes

# Designflow Core Backend API Reference

This service is the core Designflow PLM API. It handles RFQ workflows, vendors,
customers, grid layouts, admin settings, auth, files/images, comments,
notifications, tariff lookups, CBP CROSS lookup, and AI proxy calls.

In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:

```text
https://api.designflow.app/api/core/<core-backend-path>
```

Direct local base:

```text
http://localhost:5000
```

## Auth

Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
`X-User-Authorization`, and the backend remaps it to `Authorization`.

Common roles:

```text
admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
```

Routes without `authRole(...)` are either login/token routes, webhooks, or legacy
utility routes. Treat unauthenticated legacy routes as internal unless explicitly
approved for external use.

## Route Mounts

| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | `lib.router.js` | Legacy RFQ/product/vendor/grid/file endpoints |
| `/std` | `std.router.js` | Standardized product workflow |
| `/api/grid` | `grid.router.js` | Grid access metadata |
| `/api/customers` | `customer.router.js` | Customer CRUD |
| `/api/item-size` | `itemSize.router.js` | Item size CRUD |
| `/api/item_depth` | `itemDepth.router.js` | Item depth CRUD |
| `/` | `digitalocean.router.js` | File/image storage helpers |
| `/api/cross` | `cross.router.js` | CBP CROSS search proxy |
| `/api/openrouter` | `openrouter.router.js` | AI provider/model proxy |
| `/api/comment` | `comment.router.js` | Comments |
| `/api/role` | `role.router.js` | Role and UI permission metadata |
| `/api/admin` | `admin.router.js` | Admin reference data, tariffs, settings, impersonation |
| `/api/autofill` | `autofill.router.js` | Item autofill data |
| `/api/user` | `user.router.js` | User profile/photo |
| `/auth` | `auth.router.js` | Azure SSO |
| `/api/notifications` | `notification.router.js` | Notifications |
| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |

## Health and Auth

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |
| `POST` | `/auth/azure` | none | Azure SSO callback/login |
| `GET` | `/verifyToken` | user JWT | Verify current token |
| `POST` | `/verifySingleUseToken` | none | Verify single-use vendor/signup token |
| `POST` | `/signup` | none | Signup flow |
| `POST` | `/forgotPassw` | none | Forgot password flow |
| `POST` | `/changePassword` | none | Password change flow |

+# Designflow Item Master API Reference
+
+This service manages item library, item detail, licensing-status helpers, and art
+piece workflows.
+
+In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:
+
+```text
+https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/<item-master-path>
+```
+
+Direct local base:
+
+```text
+http://localhost:5003
+```
+
+## Auth
+
+Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
+`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
+`X-User-Authorization`, and the service remaps it to `Authorization`.
+
+Common roles:
+
+```text
+admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
+```
+
+## Route Mounts
+
+| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
+|---|---|---|
+| `/lib` | `item_library.router.js` | Item library |
+| `/detail` | `item_detail.router.js` | Item details and licensing status |
+| `/artPiece` | `art_piece.router.js` | Art piece library |
+| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |
+
+## Health
+
+| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |
+
+## Item Library Routes
+
+All paths below are mounted under `/lib`.
+
+| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `GET` | `/lib/getAllitems` | user JWT | Get item library rows |
+| `GET` | `/lib/getApiAllItems` | none in router | Legacy/API item list |

+
+## Item Detail Routes

+# Designflow Tracking API Reference
+
+This service handles production tracking, licensing tracking, lead-time
+calculation, and sample tracking.
+
+In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:
+
+```text
+https://api.designflow.app/api/tracking/<tracking-path>
+```
+
+Direct local base:
+
+```text
+http://localhost:5002
+```
+
+## Auth
+
+Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
+`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
+`X-User-Authorization`, and the service remaps it to `Authorization`.
+
+Common roles:
+
+```text
+admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
+```
+
+## Route Mounts

+| Mount path | Router | Purpose |

+| `/lic` | `lic.router.js` | Licensing tracking |
+| `/lead-time` | `lead-time.router.js` | Lead-time templates and calculation |
+| `/sample` | `sample.router.js` | Sample tracking |
+| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |
+
+## Health
+
+| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |
+
+## Production Tracking Routes
+

+## Licensing Tracking Routes

D:\\repos\\dflow\\designflow-item-master.content:
# Designflow Item Master API Reference

This service manages item library, item detail, licensing-status helpers, and art
piece workflows.

In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:

```text
https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/<item-master-path>
```

Direct local base:

```text
http://localhost:5003
```

## Auth

Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
`X-User-Authorization`, and the service remaps it to `Authorization`.

Common roles:

```text
admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
```

## Route Mounts

| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/lib` | `item_library.router.js` | Item library |
| `/detail` | `item_detail.router.js` | Item details and licensing status |
| `/artPiece` | `art_piece.router.js` | Art piece library |
| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |

## Health

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |

## Item Library Routes

All paths below are mounted under `/lib`.

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/lib/getAllitems` | user JWT | Get item library rows |
| `GET` | `/lib/getApiAllItems` | none in router | Legacy/API item list |
| `GET` | `/lib/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | user JWT | Get item-library grid layout |
| `POST` | `/lib/updateProdOrderHeader` | user JWT | Update production-order header |
| `POST` | `/lib/createItem` | user JWT | Create item |
| `GET` | `/lib/getItemTypes` | user JWT | Get item types |
| `GET` | `/lib/searchItemTypes` | user JWT | Search item types |
| `POST` | `/lib/createItemType` | user JWT | Create item type |
| `GET` | `/lib/getItemByNumber/:itemNumber` | user JWT | Find item by item number |
| `GET` | `/lib/getItemsByDsnRefNum/:dsnRefNum` | user JWT | Find items by design reference number |

## Item Detail Routes

All paths below are mounted under `/detail`.

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/detail/getItemInfo` | user JWT | Get item detail payload |
| `POST` | `/detail/setPrimaryImage` | user JWT | Set primary item image |
| `POST` | `/detail/updateData` | user JWT | Update item detail data |
| `POST` | `/detail/getLicenseFeedBacks` | user JWT | Get license feedback options |
| `POST` | `/detail/AddLicensingStatus` | user JWT | Add licensing status |
| `POST` | `/detail/BulkAddLicensingStatus` | user JWT | Bulk add licensing statuses |
| `POST` | `/detail/DeleteLicensingStatus` | user JWT | Delete licensing status |
| `GET` | `/detail/GetLicensingStatus/:id` | user JWT | Get licensing status by item/id |
| `POST` | `/detail/UpdateLicensingStatus` | user JWT | Update licensing status |
| `POST` | `/detail/BulkGetLicensingStatus` | user JWT | Bulk get licensing statuses |
| `POST` | `/detail/reqStyeNumber` | user JWT | Request style number |
| `POST` | `/detail/updateItemHeader` | user JWT | Update item header |
| `GET` | `/detail/GetNotifications` | user JWT | Get item-detail notifications |
| `GET` | `/detail/GetMilestones/:id` | user JWT | Get milestones |
| `POST` | `/detail/MarkMilestone` | user JWT | Mark milestone |

## Art Piece Routes

All paths below are mounted under `/artPiece`.

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/artPiece/getAllitems` | user JWT | Get art piece rows |
| `GET` | `/artPiece/getArtPiece/:id` | user JWT | Get art piece detail |
| `GET` | `/artPiece/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | user JWT | Get art-piece grid layout |
| `POST` | `/artPiece/createItem` | user JWT | Create art piece |
| `POST` | `/artPiece/setPrimaryImage` | user JWT | Set primary image |
| `POST` | `/artPiece/addAttachment/:id` | user JWT | Add art piece attachment |
| `POST` | `/artPiece/updateArtPiece` | user JWT | Update art piece |
| `GET` | `/artPiece/getProductCategories` | user JWT | Get product categories |
| `POST` | `/artPiece/updateArchive` | user JWT | Archive/unarchive art pieces |
| `POST` | `/artPiece/deleteArtPieces` | user JWT | Delete art pieces |
| `POST` | `/artPiece/checkDeleteEligibility` | user JWT | Check whether art pieces can be deleted |

## Contract Status

This is an internal application API. Several endpoints are named for existing
frontend flows and return grid-shaped data. For partner use, add stable DTO
endpoints rather than exposing these legacy route contracts directly.

# Designflow Tracking API Reference

This service handles production tracking, licensing tracking, lead-time
calculation, and sample tracking.

In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:

```text
https://api.designflow.app/api/tracking/<tracking-path>
```

Direct local base:

```text
http://localhost:5002
```

## Auth

Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
`X-User-Authorization`, and the service remaps it to `Authorization`.

Common roles:

```text
admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
```

## Route Mounts

| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | `lib.router.js` | Production tracking |
| `/lic` | `lic.router.js` | Licensing tracking |
| `/lead-time` | `lead-time.router.js` | Lead-time templates and calculation |
| `/sample` | `sample.router.js` | Sample tracking |
| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |

## Health

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |

## Production Tracking Routes

Mounted at the service root.

| Method | Path | Auth roles | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/getProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get production-order grid rows |
| `GET` | `/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get production grid layout |
| `POST` | `/updateProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Update production-order header |
| `GET` | `/factoryProdOrders` | vendor | Get factory-visible production orders |
| `POST` | `/factoryProdOrderDates` | vendor | Update factory production dates |
| `POST` | `/syncPrepackAssociations` | production | Sync prepack associations |

## Licensing Tracking Routes

All paths below are mounted under `/lic`.

| Method | Path | Auth roles | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/lic/getLic` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get licensing grid rows |
| `GET` | `/lic/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get licensing grid layout |
| `POST` | `/lic/updateLic` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Update licensing row |
| `GET` | `/lic/statuses` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get license statuses |
| `GET` | `/lic/images/:itemId` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get license image history |
| `POST` | `/lic/images` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Add license image history record |
| `GET` | `/lic/getSelectedGridView` | admin, sourcing_manager, vendor, sales, designer, production | Get selected grid view |
| `POST` | `/lic/getAllUsers` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get users for licensing flow |
| `GET` | `/lic/groups` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get all licensing groups |
| `POST` | `/lic/groups` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Create licensing group |
| `POST` | `/lic/groups/ids` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get licensing groups by ids |
| `PUT` | `/lic/groups/:id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Update licensing group |
| `DELETE` | `/lic/groups/:id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Delete licensing group |

## Lead-Time Routes

All paths below are mounted under `/lead-time`.

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/lead-time/design-team-times` | Get design-team lead-time templates |
| `GET` | `/lead-time/licensing-times` | Get licensing lead-time templates |
| `GET` | `/lead-time/factory-times` | Get factory lead-time templates |
| `POST` | `/lead-time/calculate` | Calculate lead time |
| `PUT` | `/lead-time/design-team-times/:id` | Update design-team lead-time template |
| `PUT` | `/lead-time/licensing-times/:id` | Update licensing lead-time template |
| `PUT` | `/lead-time/factory-times/:id` | Update factory lead-time template |

## Sample Tracking Routes

All paths below are mounted under `/sample`.

Role groups:

| Group | Roles |
|---|---|
| Read | admin, production, sourcing_manager, designer, sales, vendor |
| Create | admin, production, sourcing_manager, vendor |
| Write | admin, production, sourcing_manager |

| Method | Path | Auth group | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/sample/list` | Read | List samples |
| `GET` | `/sample/:id` | Read | Get sample by id |
| `POST` | `/sample` | Create | Create sample |
| `PUT` | `/sample/:id` | Write | Update sample |
| `DELETE` | `/sample/:id` | Write | Delete sample |
| `POST` | `/sample/events` | Write | Record sample check-in/out event |
| `GET` | `/sample/:id/events` | Read | List sample events |
| `GET` | `/sample/:id/comments` | Read | List sample comments |
| `POST` | `/sample/comments` | Read | Add sample comment |
| `PUT` | `/sample/comments/:id` | Read | Update sample comment |
| `DELETE` | `/sample/comments/:id` | Read | Delete sample comment |
| `POST` | `/sample/getPreSignedUrl` | Write | Create signed upload URL for sample photo |
| `POST` | `/sample/setObjectPublic` | Write | Make uploaded sample photo public |
| `GET` | `/sample/:id/photos` | Read | List sample photos |
| `POST` | `/sample/photos` | Write | Add sample photo metadata |
| `DELETE` | `/sample/photos/:attId` | Write | Delete sample photo |
| `GET` | `/sample/groups` | Read | List sample groups |
| `POST` | `/sample/groups` | Write | Create sample group |
| `GET` | `/sample/groups/:id` | Read | Get sample group |
| `PUT` | `/sample/groups/:id` | Write | Update sample group |

| `PUT` | `/sample/boxes/:id` | Write | Update sample box |
| `DELETE` | `/sample/boxes/:id` | Write | Delete sample box |
| `POST` | `/sample/boxes/:id/items` | Write | Add samples to box |
| `DELETE` | `/sample/boxes/:id/items` | Write | Remove samples from box |

## Contract Status

Production and licensing routes are internal grid-oriented application APIs.
Sample tracking has a cleaner REST-like shape and is the best starting point for
external DTO work.

This service handles synchronization flows between Designflow and Coldlion.
It is intended for system-to-system use, not direct browser or public use.

https://api.designflow.app/api/data_sync/<data-sync-path>

## Auth

```text
X-API-Key: <DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY>
```

The middleware validates this key before routing, including health checks.

## Route Mounts

| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | `lib.controller.js` | Coldlion sync endpoints |
| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |

## Health

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/api/health` | `X-API-Key` | Service health |

## Sync Routes

Mounted at the service root.

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/getItemHeaderFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Pull item headers from Coldlion |
| `GET` | `/getItemDetailFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Pull item details from Coldlion |
| `POST` | `/getItem` | `X-API-Key` | Get item information |
| `GET` | `/getMerchgroupDetailFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Pull merch group detail from Coldlion |
| `POST` | `/streamMerchgroupDetailFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Stream merch group detail sync |
| `GET` | `/getMerchGroupHeaders` | `X-API-Key` | Get merch group headers |
| `GET` | `/getProdOrderHeader` | `X-API-Key` | Pull production order headers |
| `GET` | `/getExternalVendor` | `X-API-Key` | Pull external vendors |
| `GET` | `/getExternalCustomer` | `X-API-Key` | Pull external customers |
| `GET` | `/getProdOrderDetail` | `X-API-Key` | Pull production order details |
| `GET` | `/syncPrepackAssociations` | `X-API-Key` | Sync prepack associations |

## Contract Status

This is a private integration API. Keep it service-token/API-key protected. Do
not expose these routes as a general external data API without adding stable DTOs,
rate limits, and audit logging.

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-bff\docs\system-api-inventory.md
+# Designflow API Inventory
+
+Designflow contains a multi-service API system. The Angular frontend does not
+call the backend services directly in deployed environments. It calls the BFF,
+and the BFF proxies each request to the correct backend service.
+
+## Production Entry Point
+
+| Caller | Base URL |
+|---|---|
+| Browser / frontend | `https://api.designflow.app` |
+| Local frontend | `http://localhost:5004` |
+
+The BFF exposes:
+
+```text
+/api/core/*        -> designflow-backend
+/api/item_master/* -> designflow-item-master
+/api/tracking/*    -> designflow-tracking
+/api/data_sync/*   -> designflow-data-syncing
+/health            -> BFF health
+```
+
+## Services
+
+| Repo | API role | Auth model | Local default port |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `designflow-bff` | Public API gateway for the SPA | User JWT from browser plus Cloud Run OIDC to services | `5004` |
+| `designflow-backend` | Core PLM/RFQ/admin/auth/file API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5000` |
+| `designflow-item-master` | Item library, item detail, art piece API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5003` |
+| `designflow-tracking` | Production, licensing, lead-time, sample tracking API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5002` |
+| `designflow-data-syncing` | Coldlion/system sync API | `X-API-Key` | `5001` |
+| `designflow-frontend` | API consumer, not an API provider | Sends JWT to the BFF | `4200` |
+| `infrastructure` | Deployment/IaC repo | Not checked out locally | n/a |
+
+## Important Boundary
+
+Designflow already has APIs, but they are mostly internal application APIs shaped
+around the Angular frontend. They are not yet a polished external/partner API:
+
+- no single OpenAPI spec
+- inconsistent route naming across legacy endpoints
+- many request/response bodies are raw domain rows or grid-specific payloads
+- BFF routes are stable as a gateway, but backend route contracts are not all
+  documented as public contracts
+
+For external applications, add a stable `/api/external/*` surface with DTOs.
+For AI sessions, add an MCP server that calls the existing APIs/services through
+read-only, role-aware tools.
+
+## Service API Docs
+
+| Repo | API doc |
+|---|---|
+| `designflow-bff` | `designflow-bff/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-backend` | `designflow-backend/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-item-master` | `designflow-item-master/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-tracking` | `designflow-tracking/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-data-syncing` | `designflow-data-syncing/docs/api-reference.md` |
+| `designflow-frontend` | `designflow-frontend/docs/api-consumption.md` |
+
*** Update File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-bff\README.md
@@
 | Doc | Contents |
 |---|---|
+| [`docs/system-api-inventory.md`](docs/system-api-inventory.md) | System-level API inventory across Designflow services |
 | [`docs/api-reference.md`](docs/api-reference.md) | BFF gateway routes, service keys, auth/proxy behavior |
*** End Patch

D:\\repos\\dflow\\designflow-bff\\docs\\.content:
# Designflow API Inventory

Designflow contains a multi-service API system. The Angular frontend does not
call the backend services directly in deployed environments. It calls the BFF,
and the BFF proxies each request to the correct backend service.

## Production Entry Point

| Caller | Base URL |
|---|---|
| Browser / frontend | `https://api.designflow.app` |
| Local frontend | `http://localhost:5004` |

The BFF exposes:

```text
/api/core/*        -> designflow-backend
/api/item_master/* -> designflow-item-master
/api/tracking/*    -> designflow-tracking
/api/data_sync/*   -> designflow-data-syncing
/health            -> BFF health
```

## Services

| Repo | API role | Auth model | Local default port |
|---|---|---|---|
| `designflow-bff` | Public API gateway for the SPA | User JWT from browser plus Cloud Run OIDC to services | `5004` |
| `designflow-backend` | Core PLM/RFQ/admin/auth/file API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5000` |
| `designflow-item-master` | Item library, item detail, art piece API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5003` |
| `designflow-tracking` | Production, licensing, lead-time, sample tracking API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5002` |
| `designflow-data-syncing` | Coldlion/system sync API | `X-API-Key` | `5001` |
| `designflow-frontend` | API consumer, not an API provider | Sends JWT to the BFF | `4200` |
| `infrastructure` | Deployment/IaC repo | Not checked out locally | n/a |

## Important Boundary

Designflow already has APIs, but they are mostly internal application APIs shaped
around the Angular frontend. They are not yet a polished external/partner API:

- no single OpenAPI spec
- inconsistent route naming across legacy endpoints
- many request/response bodies are raw domain rows or grid-specific payloads
- BFF routes are stable as a gateway, but backend route contracts are not all
  documented as public contracts

For external applications, add a stable `/api/external/*` surface with DTOs.
For AI sessions, add an MCP server that calls the existing APIs/services through
read-only, role-aware tools.

## Service API Docs

| Repo | API doc |
|---|---|
| `designflow-bff` | `designflow-bff/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-backend` | `designflow-backend/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-item-master` | `designflow-item-master/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-tracking` | `designflow-tracking/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-data-syncing` | `designflow-data-syncing/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-frontend` | `designflow-frontend/docs/api-consumption.md` |

Success. Updated the following files:
D D:\repos\dflow\API_INVENTORY.md

D:\\repos\\dflow\\API_INVENTORY.md.content:
# Designflow API Inventory

This workspace contains a multi-service API system. The Angular frontend does not
call the backend services directly in deployed environments. It calls the BFF, and
the BFF proxies each request to the correct backend service.

## Production Entry Point

| Caller | Base URL |
|---|---|
| Browser / frontend | `https://api.designflow.app` |
| Local frontend | `http://localhost:5004` |

The BFF exposes:

```text
/api/core/*        -> designflow-backend
/api/item_master/* -> designflow-item-master
/api/tracking/*    -> designflow-tracking
/api/data_sync/*   -> designflow-data-syncing
/health            -> BFF health
```

## Services

| Repo | API role | Auth model | Local default port |
|---|---|---|---|
| `designflow-bff` | Public API gateway for the SPA | User JWT from browser plus Cloud Run OIDC to services | `5004` |
| `designflow-backend` | Core PLM/RFQ/admin/auth/file API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5000` |
| `designflow-item-master` | Item library, item detail, art piece API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5003` |
| `designflow-tracking` | Production, licensing, lead-time, sample tracking API | User JWT via `Authorization` or `X-User-Authorization` | `5002` |
| `designflow-data-syncing` | Coldlion/system sync API | `X-API-Key` | `5001` |
| `designflow-frontend` | API consumer, not an API provider | Sends JWT to the BFF | `4200` |
| `infrastructure` | Deployment/IaC repo | Not checked out locally | n/a |

## Important Boundary

Designflow already has APIs, but they are mostly internal application APIs shaped
around the Angular frontend. They are not yet a polished external/partner API:

- no single OpenAPI spec
- inconsistent route naming across legacy endpoints
- many request/response bodies are raw domain rows or grid-specific payloads
- BFF routes are stable as a gateway, but backend route contracts are not all
  documented as public contracts

For external applications, add a stable `/api/external/*` surface with DTOs.
For AI sessions, add an MCP server that calls the existing APIs/services through
read-only, role-aware tools.

## Service API Docs

| Repo | API doc |
|---|---|
| `designflow-bff` | `designflow-bff/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-backend` | `designflow-backend/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-item-master` | `designflow-item-master/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-tracking` | `designflow-tracking/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-data-syncing` | `designflow-data-syncing/docs/api-reference.md` |
| `designflow-frontend` | `designflow-frontend/docs/api-consumption.md` |

payload.message:
Pushed to `origin/sandbox-albert` on GitHub in all six local repos:

- `designflow-backend`: `15b9a21 docs: document API surface`
- `designflow-bff`: `3e04929 docs: document API gateway`
- `designflow-frontend`: `d1134e54 docs: document API consumption`
- `designflow-tracking`: `2913034 docs: document tracking API`
- `designflow-item-master`: `b29f280 docs: document item master API`
- `designflow-data-syncing`: `5a41d4b docs: document data sync API`

I left unrelated existing dirty files alone: backend still has `AGENTS.md`, `models/db.js`, and mojibake scripts changed/untracked; data-syncing still has `models/db.js` modified.

On the MCP question: “Streamable HTTP + stdio” means **how the MCP client talks to the MCP server**.

- `stdio`: local mode. Claude/Codex/Cursor launches your MCP server as a local child process and talks through stdin/stdout. Good for developer laptops.
- `Streamable HTTP`: remote mode. The MCP server runs as a web service, like Cloud Run, and clients connect to it over HTTP. Good for shared/company-hosted Designflow MCP.

FastMCP is not an alternative to those transports. It is a framework that can run an MCP server over those transports. The official MCP docs describe stdio and Streamable HTTP as the core transports: [MCP transports](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/transports). FastMCP’s own docs say it defaults to stdio locally and can run HTTP for web services: [FastMCP server docs](https://gofastmcp.com/v2/servers/server).

Should we use FastMCP? Maybe, but with one caveat: Designflow is currently Node/Express. If we want the MCP server in Python, **FastMCP is a very good choice**. If we want it to live naturally beside the existing Node services, I’d start with the official TypeScript MCP SDK or a TypeScript FastMCP-style framework. Either way, the deployment idea stays the same: stdio for local dev, Streamable HTTP for the shared remote server.
